I'm an undergraduate mechanical engineer who spends as much time in ANSYS and on a lathe as I do in Python and OpenCV. I build vehicle systems, embedded prototypes, and AI-driven tools — then document, test, and ship them.

01 / Profile

About

PCCOE, Pune · Nashik, MH

I'm a mechanical engineering undergraduate who treats software as another manufacturing tool — something to prototype, test, and iterate with. My core work has been in vehicle dynamics, FEA-driven design, and manufacturing: brake disc optimization, suspension geometry, and composite fabrication for a Formula-Student EV.

Alongside that, I build embedded and AI-driven systems — Arduino-based prototypes, path-planning simulations, and more recently Python applications using OpenCV, MediaPipe, and search-based algorithms as part of an AI/programming internship.

The throughline is the same regardless of the tool: take a real physical or computational problem, model it, build a working version, and refine it until it holds up under testing.

Experience & Achievements

SYNTECXHUB · KOSO INDIA · FORMULA BHARAT 2025
MAY 9 – JUN 9, 2026
INTERNSHIP

AI / Programming Intern — Syntecxhub

Built Python-based AI and tooling projects, including a real-time computer vision system using OpenCV, MediaPipe, and FER for gesture and emotion recognition, an A* search-based maze solver, and a terminal chatbot powered by the Wikipedia and DuckDuckGo APIs. Work was structured for GitHub portfolio presentation and resume documentation.

JUNE 2025
INTERNSHIP

Manufacturing & Process Study Intern — KOSO India Pvt. Ltd.

Studied industrial valves used across oil & gas, petrochemical, and power industries. Operated quality control instruments including SRT, PMI, and DFT, performed non-destructive testing (LPT and MPI), and learned hydrostatic and seat leakage testing procedures. Gained hands-on exposure to Lean Manufacturing — Kaizen, Kanban, and TPM.

SEASON 2025
COMPETITION

Team Kratos Racing — Formula Bharat 2025

Competed as a Vehicle Dynamics member on the all-electric Thunderblade 5.0. The team finished AIR 1 overall — placing first across Dynamics, Skidpad, Acceleration, Autocross, Endurance, and Efficiency, with strong placements in Business Plan and Cost & Manufacturing.
